# Beudox HR - Technical Context for AI Assistants

## Architecture Overview

Beudox HR is a modern React-based frontend application built with TypeScript, using Vite as the build tool. It follows a component-based architecture with clear separation of concerns:

- **Frontend Framework**: React 18 with TypeScript for type safety
- **Build System**: Vite for fast development and optimized production builds
- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS with shadcn/ui component library for consistent UI
- **State Management**: TanStack Query for server state management and caching
- **Routing**: React Router DOM with protected routes and role-based access control
- **Backend Integration**: Supabase for authentication, database, and serverless functions

## Key Architectural Patterns

### Component Structure
- **Layout Components**: AppLayout provides consistent sidebar navigation and top bar
- **Page Components**: Each route has a dedicated page component in `/pages`
- **UI Components**: Extensive use of shadcn/ui components in `/components/ui`
- **Feature Components**: Domain-specific components (evaluations, HR policies, settings)

### Authentication & Authorization
- **Auth Provider**: Custom AuthProvider using Supabase Auth
- **Protected Routes**: Route-level protection with role-based access control
- **Role Hierarchy**: hr_manager > ceo > team_lead > employee
- **Access Control**: `canAccess()` function checks permissions based on role and route

### Data Flow
- **API Integration**: Supabase client for database operations
- **Query Management**: TanStack Query for data fetching, caching, and synchronization
- **Real-time Updates**: Supabase subscriptions for live data updates

## Business Logic & Features

### Employee Management
- Complete CRUD operations for employee records
- Profile management with avatar support
- Role assignment and department management

### Evaluation System
- **Quarterly Evaluations**: Formal performance reviews with:
  - Overall score (1-5 scale)
  - Comments and recommendations
  - Evaluator tracking
  - Role-based visibility (managers see recommendations, employees don't)
- **Daily Evaluations**: Continuous feedback with:
  - Direction tracking (received/given)
  - Real-time performance monitoring
  - Team lead and manager visibility controls
- **Timeline View**: Unified evaluation history with filtering and pagination

### HR Policies
- Rich text editing with TipTap editor
- Full formatting support (headings, lists, links, etc.)
- Policy versioning and management

### Project & Financial Management
- Project lifecycle management
- Client relationship tracking
- Invoice generation with PDF export
- Loan management system

## Database Schema (Inferred from Code)

### Core Tables
- `employees`: User profiles, roles, departments
- `evaluations`: Quarterly performance reviews
- `daily_evaluations`: Daily feedback records
- `hr_policies`: Company policies and procedures
- `projects`: Project management
- `clients`: Client information
- `invoices`: Billing and invoicing
- `loans`: Employee loan tracking

### Key Relationships
- Employees belong to companies and departments
- Evaluations link employees to evaluators
- Daily evaluations track reviewer-reviewee pairs
- Projects associate with clients and employees

## Key Files & Their Purposes

### Entry Points
- `src/main.tsx`: Application bootstrap with React rendering
- `src/App.tsx`: Main routing configuration and providers

### Core Components
- `src/components/layout/AppLayout.tsx`: Main application layout with sidebar
- `src/components/layout/AppSidebar.tsx`: Navigation sidebar with role-based menu items
- `src/components/evaluations/EvaluationTimeline.tsx`: Complex evaluation display with filtering
- `src/components/hr-policies/RichTextEditor.tsx`: Rich text editing for policies

### Utilities
- `src/lib/role-access.ts`: Role-based permission checking
- `src/lib/utils.ts`: Common utility functions (cn for class merging)
- `src/hooks/useAuth.ts`: Authentication state management

### Configuration
- `vite.config.ts`: Build configuration
- `tailwind.config.ts`: Styling configuration
- `supabase/config.toml`: Supabase project configuration

## Development Workflow

- **Local Development**: `npm run dev` starts Vite dev server
- **Testing**: Vitest for unit tests, Playwright for E2E tests
- **Linting**: ESLint with React-specific rules
- **Build**: `npm run build` creates optimized production bundle

## Environment Configuration

Required environment variables:
- `VITE_SUPABASE_URL`: Supabase project URL
- `VITE_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY`: Public Supabase API key

## Deployment Considerations

- Static site deployment (Vite builds to static files)
- Supabase handles backend services
- Environment variables must be configured for production
- Database migrations managed through Supabase

## Code Quality & Standards

- TypeScript for type safety
- ESLint for code quality
- Component composition over inheritance
- Custom hooks for shared logic
- Consistent naming conventions
- Accessibility considerations with Radix UI components
